displayTitle: By the Book: Straight-Beam to Procedure

beats.narration: In production you rarely pick your own settings. A written procedure, approved by a Level Three, spells out exactly how to run the straight-beam exam.

payload.title: BY THE BOOK
payload.sub: Execute the written procedure exactly — and know when the job no longer fits it.

beats.narration: The procedure names the search unit, frequency, couplant, scan plan, and reference standard. Change one without approval, and it is no longer the procedure.

payload.heading: Every named parameter is locked
payload.note: CHANGE ONE = LEVEL III APPROVAL FIRST

beats.narration: These control the beam. Element size sets near field and coverage; frequency trades sensitivity against grain scatter; the standard gives echo height meaning.

chips.s: Sets near field and per-pass coverage

beats.narration: Coverage is fixed too: overlap each pass ten percent, scan slowly, and watch the back wall. A fading echo can be the only flaw sign.

payload.heading: Cover the surface the way it says
payload.note: >=10% OVERLAP · SLOW · WATCH THE BACK WALL

beats.narration: So the discipline is simple. Read the procedure, match every parameter, prove sensitivity against the named standard, then record what you did.

payload.sub: Then write down exactly what you did.
